metadata.description: Singapore Chemistry curriculum covering O-Level and JC H2 A-Level, with prerequisite ordering

nodes.label: Kinetic Particle Theory
nodes.description: States of matter explained by kinetic particle theory; arrangement and movement of particles in solids, liquids and gases

nodes.label: Changes of State
nodes.description: Melting, boiling, evaporation, condensation, freezing, sublimation; heating and cooling curves

nodes.label: Diffusion
nodes.description: Diffusion in gases and liquids; factors affecting rate of diffusion; evidence for kinetic particle theory

nodes.label: Measurement & Experimental Techniques
nodes.description: Collection and measurement of gases; use of apparatus; measurement of time, temperature, mass, volume

nodes.label: Purity & Mixtures
nodes.description: Pure substances vs mixtures; criteria of purity (melting and boiling points); types of mixtures

nodes.label: Separation Techniques
nodes.description: Filtration, evaporation, crystallisation, simple and fractional distillation, paper chromatography, separating funnel

nodes.label: Elements, Compounds & Mixtures
nodes.description: Distinguishing between elements, compounds and mixtures; atoms and molecules

nodes.label: Atomic Structure
nodes.description: Protons, neutrons, electrons; proton number and nucleon number; electronic configuration (2,8,8)

nodes.label: Isotopes
nodes.description: Definition of isotopes; relative atomic mass; uses of radioisotopes

nodes.label: Ionic Bonding
nodes.description: Transfer of electrons; formation of ionic compounds; properties of ionic compounds; dot-and-cross diagrams

nodes.label: Covalent Bonding
nodes.description: Sharing of electrons; single, double, triple bonds; properties of simple and giant covalent structures

nodes.label: Metallic Bonding
nodes.description: Sea of delocalised electrons; properties of metals explained by metallic bonding

nodes.label: Writing Chemical Formulae
nodes.description: Formulae of ionic and covalent compounds; valency; polyatomic ions

nodes.label: Balancing Chemical Equations
nodes.description: Word equations; balanced symbol equations; state symbols; ionic equations

nodes.label: Mole Concept (O-Level)
nodes.description: Avogadro's number; molar mass; moles of gases at r.t.p.; concentration of solutions; stoichiometric calculations

nodes.label: Acids
nodes.description: Properties of acids; reactions with metals, bases, carbonates; strong and weak acids; H+ ions in aqueous solution

nodes.label: Bases & Alkalis
nodes.description: Properties of bases and alkalis; neutralisation; OH- ions; ammonia as a base

nodes.label: pH Scale & Indicators
nodes.description: pH scale 0-14; universal indicator; litmus; methyl orange; phenolphthalein; use of pH meter

nodes.label: Preparation of Salts
nodes.description: Soluble and insoluble salts; methods of preparation (titration, excess reagent, precipitation); solubility rules

nodes.label: Oxidation & Reduction
nodes.description: Definitions of oxidation and reduction (oxygen, hydrogen, electron transfer); oxidation states; redox equations

nodes.label: Electrolysis
nodes.description: Electrolysis of molten and aqueous compounds; selective discharge of ions; electroplating; industrial electrolysis

nodes.label: Energy Changes
nodes.description: Exothermic and endothermic reactions; energy level diagrams; bond breaking and making; activation energy

nodes.label: Rate of Reaction
nodes.description: Measuring rate; factors affecting rate (concentration, temperature, particle size, catalyst); collision theory

nodes.label: Catalysts
nodes.description: Effect of catalysts on rate; enzymes as biological catalysts; catalytic converters

nodes.label: The Periodic Table
nodes.description: Groups and periods; metals vs non-metals; relationship between electronic configuration and position

nodes.label: Group I Alkali Metals
nodes.description: Properties and reactions of lithium, sodium, potassium; trends down the group

nodes.label: Group VII Halogens
nodes.description: Properties and reactions of chlorine, bromine, iodine; displacement reactions; trends down the group

nodes.label: Group 0 Noble Gases
nodes.description: Inertness of noble gases; uses; electronic configuration with full outer shells

nodes.label: Transition Elements (O-Level)
nodes.description: Properties of transition elements; variable valency; coloured compounds; use as catalysts

nodes.label: Reactivity Series
nodes.description: Order of reactivity of metals; reactions with water, dilute acids, metal ion solutions; displacement reactions

nodes.label: Extraction of Metals
nodes.description: Extraction related to reactivity; carbon reduction; electrolytic extraction of aluminium; recycling of metals

nodes.label: Iron & Steel
nodes.description: Blast furnace; conversion of iron to steel; properties and uses of steel alloys

nodes.label: Rusting & Corrosion
nodes.description: Conditions for rusting; methods of rust prevention (galvanising, sacrificial protection, painting, oiling)

nodes.label: Alloys
nodes.description: Definition of alloys; properties compared to pure metals; examples (brass, bronze, stainless steel)

nodes.label: Composition of Air
nodes.description: Percentage composition of clean dry air; separation of gases from air by fractional distillation of liquid air

nodes.label: Air Pollution
nodes.description: Pollutants (CO, SO2, NO2, unburnt hydrocarbons, particulates); acid rain; ozone depletion; global warming

nodes.label: Carbon Cycle & Greenhouse Effect
nodes.description: Carbon cycle; greenhouse gases (CO2, CH4); enhanced greenhouse effect; climate change

nodes.label: Water
nodes.description: Water treatment and purification; tests for water; hardness of water; fluoridation

nodes.label: Fuels & Crude Oil
nodes.description: Fossil fuels; fractional distillation of petroleum; cracking; petrochemical industry

nodes.label: Alkanes
nodes.description: Homologous series; general formula CnH2n+2; naming; substitution reactions; combustion

nodes.label: Alkenes
nodes.description: General formula CnH2n; C=C double bond; addition reactions (H2, Br2, H2O, HBr); test with bromine water

nodes.label: Alcohols (O-Level)
nodes.description: Ethanol; production by fermentation and hydration of ethene; properties; combustion; social effects

nodes.label: Carboxylic Acids (O-Level)
nodes.description: Ethanoic acid; properties as weak acid; reactions; esterification

nodes.label: Esters (O-Level)
nodes.description: Formation by esterification; naming esters; uses (flavourings, solvents)

nodes.label: Polymers (O-Level)
nodes.description: Addition polymerisation (polyethene, PVC); condensation polymerisation; uses of plastics; environmental issues

nodes.label: Qualitative Analysis
nodes.description: Tests for cations (flame tests, NaOH/NH3); tests for anions (carbonate, sulfate, halides); tests for gases

nodes.label: Experimental Design
nodes.description: Planning experiments; identifying variables; collecting reliable data; drawing conclusions from data

nodes.label: Atomic Structure (H2)
nodes.description: Orbitals (s, p, d); quantum numbers; Aufbau principle; Hund's rule; Pauli exclusion; electronic configuration of atoms and ions

nodes.label: Ionisation Energy
nodes.description: Successive ionisation energies; factors affecting IE; evidence for electronic structure from IE data

nodes.label: Ionic Bonding (H2)
nodes.description: Lattice structure; lattice energy; factors affecting lattice energy; properties of ionic compounds

nodes.label: Covalent Bonding (H2)
nodes.description: Sigma and pi bonds; bond energy; bond length; dative (coordinate) bonds; VSEPR theory; shapes of molecules

nodes.label: Hybridisation
nodes.description: sp, sp2, sp3 hybridisation; relationship to molecular geometry; delocalisation of electrons

nodes.label: Intermolecular Forces
nodes.description: Van der Waals forces (instantaneous dipole-induced dipole); permanent dipole-dipole; hydrogen bonding; effect on physical properties

nodes.label: Metallic Bonding (H2)
nodes.description: Metallic bonding model; strength related to charge density and ion size; properties of metals

nodes.label: The Gaseous State
nodes.description: Ideal gas equation (pV=nRT); Dalton's law of partial pressures; deviations from ideal behaviour; real gases

nodes.label: Mole Concept (H2)
nodes.description: Stoichiometry; reacting volumes of gases; concentration; volumetric analysis; back and double titrations

nodes.label: Enthalpy Changes
nodes.description: Standard enthalpy changes (formation, combustion, neutralisation, atomisation); Hess's law; calorimetry

nodes.label: Born-Haber Cycles
nodes.description: Lattice energy; electron affinity; Born-Haber cycle construction; comparing theoretical and experimental lattice energies

nodes.label: Entropy & Gibbs Free Energy
nodes.description: Entropy; second law of thermodynamics; Gibbs free energy (G = H - TS); spontaneity of reactions; Ellingham diagrams

nodes.label: Rate Equations
nodes.description: Rate law; order of reaction (zero, first, second); rate constant; half-life; determining order from data

nodes.label: Arrhenius Equation & Catalysis
nodes.description: Arrhenius equation (k = Ae^(-Ea/RT)); effect of temperature on rate constant; homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysis; reaction mechanisms

nodes.label: Chemical Equilibria
nodes.description: Dynamic equilibrium; equilibrium constant Kc and Kp; Le Chatelier's principle; effect of conditions on equilibrium

nodes.label: Acid-Base Equilibria
nodes.description: Bronsted-Lowry theory; Ka and Kb; pH calculations for strong and weak acids/bases; buffer solutions; titration curves; indicators

nodes.label: Buffer Solutions
nodes.description: Composition of acidic and basic buffers; mechanism of buffer action; Henderson-Hasselbalch equation; biological buffers

nodes.label: Solubility Equilibria
nodes.description: Solubility product Ksp; common ion effect; predicting precipitation; relationship between Ksp and solubility

nodes.label: Redox Reactions (H2)
nodes.description: Oxidation states; balancing redox equations; disproportionation; use of oxidising and reducing agents

nodes.label: Standard Electrode Potentials
nodes.description: Standard hydrogen electrode; standard electrode potential E; electrochemical series; predicting feasibility of reactions

nodes.label: Electrochemical Cells
nodes.description: Galvanic and electrolytic cells; cell diagrams; calculating cell EMF; Nernst equation; fuel cells; batteries

nodes.label: Periodicity
nodes.description: Periodic trends in ionisation energy, electronegativity, atomic radius; variation in oxide and chloride properties across Period 3

nodes.label: Period 3 Oxides & Chlorides
nodes.description: Reactions of Na2O to P4O10 with water; bonding trends; acid-base character of oxides; Period 3 chlorides

nodes.label: Group 2 Elements
nodes.description: Reactions with water and oxygen; thermal decomposition of carbonates and nitrates; solubility trends of sulfates and hydroxides

nodes.label: Group 17 Elements
nodes.description: Trends in physical properties; oxidising ability; reactions of halogens and halide ions; disproportionation of chlorine; hydrogen halides

nodes.label: Transition Metals
nodes.description: Definition; variable oxidation states; coloured ions; catalytic activity; comparison with s-block metals

nodes.label: Complex Ions & Ligands
nodes.description: Coordinate bonding in complexes; ligands (H2O, NH3, Cl-, CN-); coordination number; shapes of complexes; ligand exchange

nodes.label: Colour of Transition Metal Complexes
nodes.description: d-d transitions; crystal field splitting; factors affecting colour (ligand, oxidation state, coordination number)

nodes.label: Reactions of Transition Metal Ions
nodes.description: Reactions of Cr, Mn, Fe, Cu, Co compounds; redox titrations (MnO4-, Cr2O72-); qualitative analysis of TM ions

nodes.label: Introduction to Organic Chemistry
nodes.description: IUPAC nomenclature; functional groups; homologous series; structural, geometric and optical isomerism

nodes.label: Isomerism
nodes.description: Structural isomerism (chain, positional, functional group); stereoisomerism (cis-trans, optical); chirality; enantiomers

nodes.label: Organic Reaction Mechanisms
nodes.description: Free radical substitution; electrophilic addition; electrophilic substitution; nucleophilic substitution (SN1, SN2); elimination

nodes.label: Alkanes (H2)
nodes.description: Free radical substitution of alkanes; combustion; unreactivity due to non-polar C-H bonds

nodes.label: Alkenes (H2)
nodes.description: Electrophilic addition reactions; Markovnikov's rule; polymerisation; oxidation with KMnO4; cis-trans isomerism

nodes.label: Arenes
nodes.description: Benzene structure and stability; electrophilic substitution (nitration, halogenation, Friedel-Crafts); side-chain oxidation

nodes.label: Halogenoalkanes
nodes.description: Nucleophilic substitution (SN1 vs SN2); elimination; relative reactivity of C-Cl, C-Br, C-I bonds; Grignard reagents

nodes.label: Alcohols (H2)
nodes.description: Classification (primary, secondary, tertiary); oxidation; dehydration; esterification; reaction with Na; distinguishing tests

nodes.label: Phenols
nodes.description: Acidity of phenol; reactions with NaOH and Na; bromination; nitration; comparison with alcohols; FeCl3 test

nodes.label: Aldehydes
nodes.description: Oxidation from primary alcohols; nucleophilic addition with HCN; reduction; Tollens' test; Fehling's test; 2,4-DNPH test

nodes.label: Ketones
nodes.description: Oxidation from secondary alcohols; nucleophilic addition; reduction; iodoform test; distinguishing from aldehydes

nodes.label: Carboxylic Acids (H2)
nodes.description: Acidity; reactions with bases, carbonates, alcohols; reduction; acyl chloride formation; relative acid strength

nodes.label: Acyl Chlorides
nodes.description: Nucleophilic substitution with water, alcohols, ammonia, amines; more reactive than carboxylic acids

nodes.label: Esters (H2)
nodes.description: Formation from acids/acyl chlorides; hydrolysis (acid and base); uses; transesterification; fats and oils

nodes.label: Amines
nodes.description: Classification; basicity; preparation from halogenoalkanes and nitriles; reactions with acyl chlorides; comparison of aliphatic vs aromatic amines

nodes.label: Amides
nodes.description: Formation from acyl chlorides and amines; hydrolysis; Hofmann degradation; properties of the amide bond

nodes.label: Amino Acids
nodes.description: Amphoteric nature; zwitterions; isoelectric point; electrophoresis; peptide bond; optical activity

nodes.label: Proteins
nodes.description: Primary, secondary, tertiary, quaternary structure; denaturation; hydrolysis of proteins; enzymes

nodes.label: Nitriles
nodes.description: Formation from halogenoalkanes; hydrolysis to carboxylic acids; reduction to amines; use in carbon chain extension

nodes.label: Addition Polymers
nodes.description: Formation from alkenes; examples (polyethene, PVC, polystyrene, PTFE); properties and uses; environmental impact

nodes.label: Condensation Polymers
nodes.description: Polyesters (from diols and dicarboxylic acids); polyamides (nylon, Kevlar); hydrolysis of condensation polymers

nodes.label: Organic Synthesis & Deduction
nodes.description: Multi-step synthesis pathways; retrosynthetic analysis; functional group interconversions; deducing structures from reactions and spectra
